sglang.title: Fix a regression introduced by overlapping KV cache writing
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Fix a perf regression on throughput introdcued by #4356

sglang.title: Improve DP attention
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Use a better padding strategy for cuda graph. If TP=8, DP=8, when batch size = 1, the previous implementation will pad it to global batch size 8. The new implementation will allow running global batch size 1, so it is faster at low bs range.  It is 1.15x faster then the old implementation for deepse

sglang.title: Fix Llama3.3 tool call support
sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Llama3.3 frequently fails to generate the <|python_tag|> token at higher temperatures, so additional checks are needed to properly detect tool calls.

Modifications
This commit refactors the tool call detection logic to allow each tool call parser to handle its own detection logic:

Added

sglang.title: Fix the output of hidden states after HTTP requests
sglang.author: Qiaolin-Yu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Previsously, the output of the hidden states will become empty after HTTP requests. This is because the hidden states in the JSON object are tensors, causing compatibility issues. This PR unifies the output's hidden states to use List[List[float]].

sglang.title: Hierarchical Caching supports MLA
sglang.author: zeroorhero
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
I am deeply grateful to @xiezhq-hermann for implementing the Hierarchical Caching feature, which has expanded the storage capacity of the KV cache. However, the version only supports MHA and not MLA.  This PR introduces support for Hierarchical Caching in the context of MLA.
At present, t

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix chunked prefill for GGUF
vllm.author: SzymonOzog
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As described in the comment, in chunked prefill the input to logits processor can be of shape (0,  hidden_size)
The way I'm fixing it now is a bit of a hack, open to suggestions for a better fix

vllm.title: [CI/Build]  Delete ultravox LoRA test
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The lora test for this model has issues:

It requires downloading 2 models.
It doesn't actually test by training a LoRA model.

Delete the lora test for this model to reduce CI testing pressure.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] fix benchmark moe
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Introduced by #14068

vllm.title: [VLM] Support pan-and-scan for Gemma3 multi-modal processor
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Support pan-and-scan image processing for Gemma 3 models. (follow-up to #14660)

Please note that you need to pass --hf-overrides '{"do_pan_and_scan": true}' to enable this.

Add correctness tests for Gemma 3 model.

Note: This is not enabled in CI yet because of the older transformers version used

vllm.title: [VLM] Support loading InternVideo2.5 models as original InternVLChatModel
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #14180
Separate from #14688

Note that hierarchical token compression (HiCo) technology newly added in InternVideo hasn't supported yet, so the outputs will be different from HF's currently.
Hierarchical token compression (HiCo) will be added after video support landed.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix prompt format of GLM4V
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix the text representation of the image in online serving and tests to be consistent with #13157. Also fix the error message for passing --hf-overrides to be JSON-compatible.
@jeejeelee can you help build the chat template to use in online serving? https://huggingface.co/THUDM/glm-4v-9b doesn't wor

vllm.title: [V1][Core] using cached vocab_size for Structured Outputs
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, we obtained vocab_size for xgrammar from hf_text_config directly.
However, in the recent version of xgrammar, the detected vocab_size now include special_tokens, in which raises the issue found in #14534
By calculating the vocab size, it ensures supporting custom tokenizers with the like

vllm.title: [V1] Detokenizer: Respect Stop Tokens + `not include_stop_str_in_output`
vllm.author: afeldman-nm
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR plumbs the engine core finish_reason into the incremental_detokenizer.update() method. The invariant is that if finish_reason == STOP in the engine core output, the engine core must have detected a token-based stop condition (EOS or stop-token.) The detokenizer will truncate the most recent

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR addresses typographical errors found across all Markdown (.md) files in the repository. Specifically, the following was done:

Used an LLM-based approach (Gemini) to automatically scan all Markdown files for obvious typos and grammatical mistakes.
Manually reviewed the LLM suggestions to con

vllm.title: forward fix PR 14245, restore build on ROCm 6.2
vllm.author: jeffdaily
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
PR #14245 broke compilation for ROCm 6.2.
__hip_fp8_e4m3 only exists starting in ROCm 6.3. PR #14245 added VLLM_DISPATCH_FP8_TYPES that on ROCm instantiates both OCP and FNUZ kernels. We need to replace the new __hip_fp8_e4m3 HW cvt with something reasonable that doesn't rely on the ROCm 6.3 feature

vllm.title: [V1] Move OOM check into sampler run
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Move the OOM check from model warmup into _dummy_sampler_run since OOM can also happen during profile_run.
FIXES #12529

vllm.title: [V1] Temporarily disable FlashInfer Rejection Sampler
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Our FlashInfer-based rejection sampling is unstable. This PR disables it temporarily.

vllm.title: [Kernel] [V1] Further optimizations to ROCm (Triton) Backend to better handle GQA.
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt: TLDR: This PR adds some further optimizations to chunked_prefill_paged_decode op to better handle models with GQA. Serving benchmarks using V1 indicate that with these changes, we see a 25% improvement in throughput for llama3.1-8b on an H100 vs. the current Triton implementation. With these changes

vllm.title: [Neuron] flatten test parameterization for neuron attention kernels
vllm.author: liangfu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR include two changes:

Split the test scripts into 1_core and 2-core test folders
Refactored test_contexted_kv_attention to flatten the nested parameterization (3 loops → 1 list) while expanding coverage variety. The change reduces test combinations from 64 to 10 carefully selected cases, ach

vllm.title: [Feature] Add visionarena offline support for benchmark_throughput
vllm.author: JenZhao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a follow-up PR for #14036 to support vision arena in the benchmark_throughput script.
With the vllm-chat backend, the prompt token count will now include image token counts, as we will be using the request output to perform the calculation.
CLOSES #14600

hf: lmarena-ai/vision-arena-bench-v0

vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #14771 refactored how we retrieve the model id but broke main CI. This PR fixes it.

vllm.title: [Attention] MLA get rid of materialization
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Based on these calculations:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/17eoqEbhblvtNsRRlFSjCQnEXZiBxtLgZGKD4IgZUz38/edit?usp=sharing
It's actually better to just not materialize the absorbed W_Q_UK and W_UV_O as it reduces memory usage (and total flops) and instead compute using sequential matmuls. One

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Kernel][CPU] Fix num_tokens in CPU rotary embedding kernel
vllm.author: gau-nernst
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
num_tokens was computed incorrectly in the CPU kernel of rotary embedding. I found this issue while working on MLA kernel for CPU backend, which led to segfault due to out-of-bounds memory access.
Reference to the CUDA kernel
